scientific_integrity.detail: This skill produces structured calculation or lookup outputs rather than empirical research findings, so scientific integrity review is not applicable.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill operates within specialized research tooling scope and does not generate clinical or diagnostic decision outputs.

methodological_ground.detail: No experimental methodology claims are made; the skill generates deterministic structured calculation or lookup outputs without asserting scientific conclusions.

code_usability.detail: Any code produced by this skill is utility-oriented, not bioinformatics analysis code; research veto code review is not applicable.

functional_suitability.note: Region Alias Mapping section added with IANA timezone mapping table. preferred_hours parameter added. Covers overlap recommendations, local-time mapping, DST notes, and tradeoff analysis.

reliability.note: Region alias normalization now documented. Error handling documented; missing-field prompts present; DST limitation explicitly noted.

performance_context.note: References directory present with guidelines.md and audit-reference.md; good progressive disclosure.

agent_usability.note: Workflow clear; Region Alias Mapping table provides clear normalization guidance. Stress-case rules defined; feedback design good.

human_usability.note: Description is highly discoverable for distributed team coordinators; forgiveness improved — region aliases now documented.

security.note: No credentials required; input validation present; no sensitive data exposure risk.

maintainability.note: Clean structure with references/ directory; Region Alias Mapping table makes normalization rules easy to update.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision good; progressive disclosure via references/; composability still limited — no structured output schema; idempotency strong for pure scheduling logic.

key_strengths: Region Alias Mapping section with IANA timezone table resolves the v1 ambiguity gap — US/EU/Asia aliases now explicitly documented
preferred_hours parameter added for per-region scheduling constraints, expanding the skill's practical utility
Clean two-parameter input model (regions + duration) is minimal and immediately usable
Explicit DST limitation in Scope Boundaries prevents false confidence in time calculations

recommendations.title: No structured output schema for downstream calendar tool integration
recommendations.problem: The skill outputs human-readable text but provides no structured JSON output option, limiting integration with calendar tools or scheduling automation.
recommendations.root_cause: Output format is not parameterized; no --format flag or output schema is documented.
recommendations.fix: Add a --format flag (text/json) and document the JSON output schema in SKILL.md or references/ to enable downstream integration with calendar APIs.

recommendations.title: No guidance on handling more than 3 regions or very large team distributions
recommendations.problem: The usage example shows three regions but provides no guidance on how the skill handles 5+ regions where no overlap window may exist.
recommendations.root_cause: The skill does not document a 'no viable window' fallback or guidance for extreme timezone spread scenarios.
recommendations.fix: Add a 'No Viable Window' fallback to the workflow: if no overlap exists within business hours for all regions, output the least-bad option with explicit tradeoff notes and suggest async alternatives.
